Hey everyone, this is Russell again, welcome to the third pre-training video. And this is one, I want to pre-empt this one, because I know where you guys are going to get stuck. I've been doing this now for a decade and a half. I know where I got stuck, I know where other people got stuck, and if I can help navigate that before we jump right in, it's going to make all of our jobs so much easier and get into the finish line way faster. So the first part of this is a concept I actually learned from Dan Sullivan, who's the owner of a company called Strategic Coach, and he's got tons of really cool frameworks and ideas around business and entrepreneurship. But this is one that had a big impact on me, and I think it's going to be super big for most of you guys. And the concept is, it's called the who, not the how. And so kind of the way this works is, one day you guys woke up, right, and you had a vision. So here's you, and you're all excited because you're like, this is going to be the most amazing thing in the world. And you see your vision, your vision's up here, right? I'm going to do the vision in red. So you have this vision of where you want to go, what you want to create, and how you want to serve people, right? So here's your big vision, and you see it, right? You see like, there's a vision, like that's what I want to create, okay? And hopefully you guys have all experienced that. But if you haven't, finding people who are visionary to surround yourself with, and a lot of times you can partner with them, but most of us have this kind of vision, right? Like, this is what I want to do, this is what I want to go. Like, I want to help people lose weight, I want to help people make money, I want to help people, blah, blah, blah, whatever your thing is, right? Everyone's got something different. Be in this vision of what you want to create, okay? And what happened, you started down the path and the process of going down here, you're going down this path, and then something happened, right? You're learning, you're going through all of studying, you're having a bunch of fun, and you're going down this path, and all of a sudden, ugh, you get stuck. You learn something and you're like, how do I do that? Okay, how many of you guys have felt that ever, especially in the funnel building world? Like, how do I do that? I need a logo, I don't know how to do a logo, how do I do that? I don't know how to upload an image, I don't know how to edit an image, I don't know how to edit a podcast, I don't know how, how, how all these hows start coming, right? So what happens, all of a sudden you get derailed by this big huge how, like, how? Question mark, like, ugh, I don't know exactly how to do this, right? How many of you guys have felt that before? And what happens with how, when you hit a how, like, how do I do this? Then you get this rabbit hole, like, studying and learning and trying to figure it out, and trying to learn Photoshop and trying to learn this and that, and that, and all of a sudden, like, takes you on this huge rabbit trail, and then what happens is on entrepreneurs. Okay, entrepreneurs are driven by momentum, okay? If you listen to Alex Sharfin at all, one of our two Comic-Con X coaches, entrepreneurs are driven by momentum, moving forward or moving backwards, like, that's how we judge things, if we're doing well, if this means we're moving forward. So we're moving forward towards our vision, and then we hit these hows, and we get overwhelmed, we get stressed, and then something bad happens. We move into this zone over here that we call procrastination. Procrastination, it's almost like a swamp, like, it's this dirty thing where you get stuck, like, quicksand, you can't move through it, okay? And so a lot of times, entrepreneurs get stuck in procrastination. A lot of times they think, oh, I'm just procrastinating, so I don't know what to do, and then we get in these things where we believe it's procrastination, but the reason why we're procrastinating is because we're trying to figure out the hows that are distracting us from our vision, and our vision, what it actually got us here, and it's moving us forward. How many of you guys have felt that before? If you've ever gotten stuck in this procrastination, this is why you're getting stuck with the how. Now, I want you to understand that for people like us, we don't need to know all the hows all the time. Like, how many of you guys think I know how to run Facebook ads? I don't. How many of you guys think I know how to edit an image in Photoshop? I don't. How many of you guys think I know how to run a video camera? I don't. I don't know most of the hows of this business, yet I've been super successful, and the reason why, and this is what Dan Sullivan talked about, he said, instead of asking yourself, how do I do this, you have to ask yourself a different question. You ask different questions, you get different answers. So instead of asking me, how do I do this, you just stop and say, who, who already knows how to do this? Who's that person that already knows how to do it, that I can just find them? Okay. And you get the right who, they will take you to your vision very, very quickly. Okay. So a couple examples of this. How many of you guys out there have ever built a house or been part of the process? Right? It's funny when like in, in, in, in that world, people don't go and say, I'm a contractor. I'm going to build a house. And then the guys out there, like digging your hole and then he's like trying to glue pipes together. And he's like laying foundation and then like all things right. No, what happens is the dude who's building your house. So here's a house. The guy you hire, my awesome looking house is what we call a contractor. Right? He's a contractor. What does the contractor do? He has the blueprints of the house. He didn't even create those. He hired some new blueprint. He gets the blueprints. He's like, okay, to build this house, I know I need a couple of people. And he pulls out his Rolex. He's like, I need somebody right here to do the plumbing. I need somebody to do the heating. I need somebody to do the lights. I need somebody to do the foundation. Somebody to do the framing. He has all these hoos. That's all a contractor does. He's got a Rolex right here, the whole bunch of hoos. That's it. Right? Most of the contractors I don't, that I know, and I apologize if any of them watch this right now, most of them aren't that talented. Right? They have a Rolex, a bunch of hoos, and they get a deal. They sell some of the house to look at the Rolex. Like, okay, cool. I need this guy, this guy, this guy, and the plug in a bunch of hoos. And then the house is built because the vision's done. Right? That's what happens. Okay? But for some reason, us entrepreneurs, we start deciding we want to change the world, something way more complicated than building a house. And instead of saying, who in the people can help me on my journey? We sit back and say, how do I do this? I got to learn graphic design because I need a logo. And we spent the next 10 years of our life trying to learn Photoshop. And we finally, like, do a design and it's horrible. And then we're like, okay, logo's done. Whoo, I made it through this procrastination circle. Now you're six months into your business, like, okay, now I need a sales letter. Okay, I got to learn copywriting. So you read the copywriting books and the things. And you go in the copywriting forums and you start studying and learning and mastering. And like, eventually six months later, you're in the right copy and you write a sales letter that's like, eh, okay. Right? Okay, cool. Now I need a funnel. So you're like, okay, I got to learn funnel. So you learn funnel strategy. And you go to thing by thing by thing by thing. And a year later, two years later, five years later, 10 years later, you never get to your vision ever. Okay? Because you're so focused on the how, how, how, and you're trying to learn and understand it. Okay? And so for you guys, that's why most of you guys are struggling. And I'm like, okay, I'm going to start learning these because you're so focused on the how, instead of focusing on the whose. Okay? So for me, I look back in time, like, when I was building my company initially, I had no skills, which I'm actually really grateful for now. So this is Russell 15 years ago, right? And so I was like, I need a funnel. And then they're like, okay, you need a logo. I'm like, I don't know how to do a logo. And so I went and there's different sites I used to go to back in the day. And I'm going to give you a resource that's better than all of them. But there's a bunch of sites we used to go to. I started hiring designers. I hired tons of designers, like 10, 15 designers. And from that, I found like three or four that I really, really liked. So I built my own little Rolex right here of designers. Okay, here's my three favorite designers. Okay. And it was exactly about how much they charge for things. And I knew. And I said, like, I need to learn to copy. I'm like, I can't learn to copy. I'm in school. I'm wrestling. I don't have time for that. So when I found copyrights, I found like how much copyrights I hired for different projects. I found two or three that I really liked. I had some copywriters. And I'm like, I need people who could do programming and computers. I started doing that. And eventually I built this huge Rolex of people that could do a big Rolex of all the hoos. Who are all the people I need to get this thing done. Okay. And then what would happen for me is I was sitting in my college classroom and I was sitting there trying to pay attention to my teacher, which was really hard for me because I got severe ADD and a whole bunch of other things. I'm sitting there, trying to listen, trying to listen. All of a sudden, oh, I had this vision, right? The vision came. I was like, oh my gosh, if I created this, like people would love it. It would serve my money. They'd be like, oh, the vision happens, right? I started going down this thing. And then guess what? I wasn't smart enough to figure out any of the house. So instead I put my Rolex and I'd say, okay, cool. I emailed this guy and I can tell you their names if you wanted. I'm not going to tell you their names, but I'll tell you. So I can like, okay, Rob's going to my logo. So message Rob on Facebook message. Hey man, I got an idea. Here's the domain. Can you make a logo? He writes back five minutes later. Yep, dude. Here's a link. I send him some money. Boom. I'm like, okay. I need a headline. I need a good copy. Hey, can you write a headline for this? This is the constant I'm trying to do. Boom, writes headline, sends it back. Okay, I need a video. I need this. And I would just message all the people I needed to hit my vision. Then they go and create all this stuff. I'd get it back and I didn't even know how to like plug it all together. So then I had hired, I had another guy who was like, hey, you're full-time jobs. I got all the assets back. You create all the different pages. You plug in the videos. You plug in the things. And that was it. And I started creating these things. Okay. If you look at my journey last 15 years, we've launched probably conservatively, I don't know, 200 plus different offers I've created in the last 10 years. And it came off of that process. Okay. It's funny. ClickFunnels was one of the main reasons why I built ClickFunnels, because I had such a big team of people who have to do everything for me, because I couldn't do it. ClickFunnels made it simple where I could actually do that piece of it, which by the way, now is my favorite piece. So I like, I love doing that piece. But for most part, we spend so much time trying to figure out how we never actually get to the vision. Okay. And when you guys understand, you got to shift that focus from the how to the who. Okay. And one thing I want to talk about before I kind of show you guys where to find all the who's that you're going to need to make this funnel and this vision live. It's interesting. When I was in college, I didn't learn a lot of really useful things, but there's one concept I learned that was huge for me. It was the concept of opportunity cost. Opportunity cost. Okay. So for any of the kids watching, this is your college education. This is the most important thing I learned from six years in school. Okay. And no, I had not a doctor. I went to a lot of years because I was wrestling and it took a long time. Right. So opportunity cost. This is the key. So what is the opportunity cost? Okay. Well, if you look at the definition, the opportunity cost is the benefit or the value of something that must be given up to acquire or achieve something else. Okay. So what does that mean? What it means for me and for you is let's say you want to go get a logo designed, right? But you don't know how to design logos. So you go and you're like, I got to learn Photoshop. You spend 30 hours learning how to use Photoshop and how to move things around. You spend 30 hours of your time to go and create that, right? So I think, well, what's the value of my time and everyone values their time differently. So let's say you value your time at $100 an hour, right? And you spent 30 hours doing that. That means you spent $3,000 to go and actually create that. So that was the cost that it cost you to actually go and create that logo. But the opportunity cost is like, what could you have done with those 30 hours instead? Right. So whenever I have anything, any project I'm trying to do, I look at the opportunity cost I'm saying, okay, if I'm designing logos, it's going to take me 10 hours. I value my time at, let's say, $100 an hour, $3,000. Can I get somebody to do a logo for less than $3,000? If so, I'm going to do it because it's the same physical cost and money, right? But then the other side is then those other 10 hours I have back to go do something different. Something I'm really, really good at that I can go and I can actually focus on. And so for you guys, when you're thinking about that, every single time you're doing something, it's figuring out what's the opportunity cost to me trying to figure out how to do Photoshop or how to edit this webpage or how to write a copy or do those things. Like some things you're going to have to learn, right? But the most part, you're going to be able to outsource most of those things and find people who are already amazing at it. Even if I spent the 30 hours learning Photoshop, I'm still not going to be nearly as good of a designer as someone who spent the last 30 years of their life doing it. It might only take them an hour. They can bust it out and they're going to charge me $100. So why would you not do that? Okay, and I see this in people's lives, not just in the online business. I see it all the time. I see my parents, my friends, my family members who will go and they'll be working at the office making $150, $200, $500 an hour or whatever and they go home and they mold their own lawn. I'm like, you can pay someone $20 an hour to mold your lawn. Like why would like the opportunity cost if you do it, like those two hours you took to mold lawn, you could have spent that time making money at your job or creating your next thing or whatever that thing might be. And so if you don't want, you're always remembering opportunity costs because that's the key that's going to free you as you're building these things out. So I want you to understand this concept of opportunity costs because this is what's going to give you guys the ability to justify you finding the who's. Okay? A lot of you guys will struggle in this challenge because you're going to try to figure out how to do every single thing and because of that, you're not going to be as successful as you could be. Okay? So do not worry about the how of everything. I'm going to show you guys the vision of how this thing works and then like, this is awesome Russell. Who's the person I need to find? Tell me to fulfill that piece of the vision. Who do I need to find to help me lay out the funnel? Who do I need to find to do whatever? Now if there's pieces you like, then feel free to do them. But other everything else, you should be outsourcing. Everything else that you're not amazing, it should be outsourcing. So where do you outsource it to? Well, we created something, I told you before that I had built up my own Rolodex of funnel building people. And so recently we created something called the funnel Rolodex. And what the funnel Rolodex was, it was actually selfishly something I built for myself initially. I said, I want to find, I want to build a Rolodex of all of the best graphic designers, all the best funnel strategy people, all the best copywriters, all the best email copywriters, all the best everybody and put them in this big Rolodex. And I have projects, I don't have to be like, how am I going to learn this? I can be like, who's the best to go over there, scroll through the Rolodex, find someone who I can justify the opportunity cost of paying them, pay for them to do it and have it done. And so we built up this Rolodex that has designers through all your logos, that has copywriters, do videos, has all the different things that you're looking for that you're going to need in this process, they're all there. So as you're going through this process, I want you stressing out less about the how, because if you do the how, you're stuck in procrastination and we only have 30 days to change the world. And if you're stuck in procrastination, we're not going to make it past these 30 days. Do you understand that? Okay. So you have to be able to come back and say, okay, I can't, I can't spend, I'm going to be stuck in procrastination. I got to figure out who are the who's. So as I do each of these modules, be like, okay, who do I need to help me do this? I need a designer. I need someone to edit my podcast. I need somebody to do this. Okay. And if you're like, Russell, I have no money right now, I can't do that. Then what I'd say is like, look at, again, it comes back to this, go get a job, spend those five hours earning the money you need to hire that person, because it'll probably do a better job than you. I'm not allowed these things anyway, because they're specialists. Like the logo guys have been doing logos their whole life. Okay. So I would go, go to your job, work for five hours, take that money, pay that dude over here, right? Or go find something, go sell it on eBay. Like I literally started my, my business by finding out how much the who's needed for money. And I would go earn that money and then come back and give it to him to do that prior to the project. I would sell stuff on eBay. I would rate leaves on the weekends, like whatever it took to get the money to pay the who's to help me create my vision. Okay. And that's the big secret, you guys. Any of you guys think that you're going to become the contractor who does everything you're going to fail. Okay. But any of you guys understand that the way that this is built is you are the funnel contractor. Okay. You are the contractor of your business and your job now is to start building out your old X of who are all the people I need to build this vision. Okay. All you need to know as a contractor is how the, like all you need to have for you as the entrepreneurs have the vision. This is the vision. This is what I want to create. This is what it looks like. I'm going to give you guys the strategy to have the framework and you have, um, basically the blueprints, what you need. Then you got to go and find all the who's to go and do all those different pieces for you. Does that make sense? And, um, and the fun thing about this, um, I know that some of you guys are going to geek out on different parts. Some of you guys are already good designers. Some of you guys are already good copywriters or, um, uh, in quick phones, things like that. Like what I would recommend doing for, if I was you is go and submit to be inside the Rolex. Let's say you are the best logo designer in the world. Go in there and design logos for other people. Okay. Go and do logos for a hundred bucks a logo and do 10 of them. Now you got a thousand bucks and take that thousand bucks to hire some of your video sales letter. Right. Like that's how you should be thinking through these things. I had to go rake leaves when I was a kid to do that. You can do it now just literally by going in the Rolex and figuring out the piece of year, the best step. Okay. Most people are good at one piece of this whole thing. Um, in fact, I have a guy, uh, he's really good at writing headlines. In fact, he wrote the sub headline for my book right here. It says, uh, find your message, build a tribe and change the world. So I pay him a hundred dollars, try to headline for me. I tell him, here's the thing. I need a headline. I pay him a hundred dollars and you'll write out five or six variations and give them to me. I'm like, sweet. And I take those headlines and I use them. So I use the headline that he wanted them once you wrote right there. Okay. So here he makes a hundred bucks headline, which is kind of, he's probably the most expensive headline writer I know, but that's awesome. Right. So if I was him, I'd go in the Rolex. I would have a hundred dollars for headline and go and do 10, 20, 30 headlines for people make two, three, four thousand dollars. Okay. Because you're the who for someone else's business and then reinvest that in the who's you need for your business. Okay. This funnel Rolex can and should be funding and financing this entire your entire vision for you. Okay. You're going to figure out what, which, which of the micro pieces of this are you the best at geek out and become better and better and better and better. Just that one piece and use that to raise the money now to do, to hire people, do the other pieces like, um, that way this whole ecosystem is funding itself and you don't have to go to pocket if you don't want to. Okay. So that helps. But that's the biggest thing I want you guys to understand, um, especially in this finite, uh, challenge where we've got 30 days to do this. You only have 30 days to, um, to do it. So if you get stuck in trying to figure everything out, it's how to do it. You're going to stuck in here in procrastination. You're never going to make it to the end. But if you focus on figuring out the who's to get your vision to happen, that's the magic. That's what's going to happen. Okay. All right. So that said, you guys, um, what your homework is for tonight is just go to the funnel Rolex and right now I don't want you to hire anybody wanting to go look through it and go look at, um, the different people, look at providers, like how much the logos cost on average and everyone's got a range. So people charge more, something will charge less, how much does copyrighting, how much does email, how much is it, like all the different pieces and look at like, are there any of these things that I'm good at? Like anything that I could do, if there's something you're good at, go and submit yourself and try to get inside, inside of the Rolex. Okay. Um, and, uh, and some of you guys aren't going to want to be service providers, those who are like, it's a great way to start leveraging this community, start making money and you can then reinvest in the other pieces. You need the other who's and it's really powerful, uh, system. So that's our recommended, uh, right now is go play in the Rolex, have some funds, see the ecosystem we've been building for you and see if you want to plug into it, but now you're going to find out exactly who all your who's are. And this is going to make your job so much easier as soon as you get this, these blueprints from me for your house. Then you've got to go plug it in and get the people and pretty soon your vision will be complete and you're going to have something amazing. So thanks so much.
